headline: How Carnival’s Profit Rebound, Buyback and Fuel Cost Concerns At Carnival Corporation (CCL) Has Changed Its Investment Story
summary: Carnival Corporation & plc reported past first-quarter 2026 results with revenue rising to US$6,165 million and net income improving to US$258 million, turning a prior-year loss into a profit. The company paired this earnings beat with a new US$2.50 billion share buyback and a raised operational outlook, while warning that sharply higher fuel costs could materially affect full-year results. We’ll now explore how Carnival’s return to profitability amid rising fuel costs could influence the...

headline: Carnival Corporation (CCL) Valuation Check After Recent Share Price Weakness
summary: Why Carnival Corporation & (CCL) is Back on Investor Radars Carnival Corporation & (CCL) is drawing attention after a mixed run in its share price, including a 4.3% drop over the past day and weaker returns over the past month and past 3 months. For context, the cruise operator now carries a market value of about US$34.9b, with reported revenue of US$26.6b and net income of US$2.8b. This offers investors a sizable, profit-generating travel business to evaluate. See our latest analysis for...

summary: Rising oil prices are pressuring the cruise operator.

headline: Carnival Reports Record Q1 Sales: Are Cruise Stocks Oversold?
summary: Although Carnival (CCL) is off to a strong start to the year, the company acknowledged challenges from rising fuel costs and geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.
